content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

All four amendments target concrete reconciliation correctness and robustness regressions in terminal final-review handling, with clear failure modes and practical fixes; I accept all proposed changes.

## Votes

- `fr-3-bce5a1b1`: Accept
  Limiting terminal reconciliation to only the latest FinalReview aggregate can silently drop deferred non-fix findings from standard review stages and earlier-round findings, so state can be incomplete and post-run actions (like coverage tracking/comments) can miss required items. Processing all committed review/final_review findings with per-round latest selection is the correct model.
- `fr-3-6e395bc0`: Accept
  Using canonical amendment row count for threshold checks undercounts multi-source duplicates because duplicate finding content is merged into one canonical amendment. Counting underlying source/finding occurrences preserves the intended quorum semantics and avoids false negatives at threshold boundaries.
- `fr-3-dc280e72`: Accept
  Suppressing comment/sync failures after a target bead is found creates silent partial success: reconciliation can return success without durable idempotent comment state. Returning an error for these mutation/flush failures is necessary for reliability and retryability.
- `fr-3-0587662a`: Accept
  Substring matching on comment text can incorrectly deduplicate by matching unrelated content containing the amendment id token, causing missed linking comments and hidden reconciliation steps. This is a concrete false-positive risk and should be replaced with a structured exact-match check.
